Strange Places

The Underpass Survey Marker That Changes Sides

The Underpass Survey Marker That Changes Sides follows an underpass survey marker appears on different sides of the road in successive inspection records, showing how ordinary paperwork can become the center of a legend, mystery, or symbolic tradition.

Strange Places - Place Record Legend - 10 min read - Updated Jul 17, 2026
Strange Places

The Underpass Survey Office Map Note Pointing to a Removed Door

The Underpass Survey Office Map Note Pointing to a Removed Door follows a map note from underpass survey office points toward a marker that changes sides between reports through a door removed from later plans, showing how ordinary paperwork can become the center of a legend, mystery, or symbolic tradition.

Strange Places - Place Record Legend - 8 min read - Updated Jul 18, 2026
Strange Places

The Underpass With Two Different Street Names

An underpass whose signs name two streets depending on which direction a person enters from.

Strange Places - Road Place - 8 min read - Updated Jul 6, 2026
Strange Places

The Unused Ferry Office Index Card for an Unlisted Shelf

The Unused Ferry Office Index Card for an Unlisted Shelf follows an index card from unused ferry office points to a shelf where a pier visible only in old tide notes should be stored, though no shelf is listed, showing how ordinary paperwork can become the center of a legend, mystery, or symbolic tradition.

Strange Places - Place Record Legend - 10 min read - Updated Jul 18, 2026
Strange Places

Tower of London Ghosts: Royal Memory, Execution Stories, and a Haunted Landmark

Tower of London ghost stories attach haunting traditions to a real historic landmark associated with imprisonment, executions, royal power, and public memory.

Unexplained Mysteries

Amber Room Mystery: The Lost Treasure That Still Refuses a Final Ending

The Amber Room was an ornate chamber of amber panels and gold decoration that disappeared during World War II. A reconstruction exists, but the fate of the original remains disputed.

Unexplained Mysteries - Lost Treasure Mystery - 9 min read - Updated Jul 19, 2026
Unexplained Mysteries

D.B. Cooper Hijacking: Ransom, Parachute, and the Unsolved Man in the Suit

D.B. Cooper is the name attached to the unknown man who hijacked Northwest Orient Flight 305 on November 24, 1971, received $200,000 in ransom, and parachuted from the aircraft into the night. His identity and fate remain unresolved.

Unexplained Mysteries - Unsolved Case - 9 min read - Updated Jul 19, 2026
Unexplained Mysteries

Devil’s Sea Mystery: Dragon’s Triangle, Maritime Rumor, and the Dangerous Waters of Legend

Dyatlov Pass Incident: Timeline, Evidence Limits, and Why the Mystery Lasts

A source-aware Kyunolab record tracing the 1959 Ural Mountains case whose official records, injuries, weather, and later theories remain widely debated.

Flying Dutchman Folklore: The Ghost Ship, the Horizon, and a Maritime Legend

The Flying Dutchman is a maritime legend about a ghost ship condemned to sail the seas and appear on the horizon.
